We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure the timely reclassification of completed projects from the Construction in Progress account to the appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment accounts immediately upon project completion, as required under Section 50, Chapter 3 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I; and
Recognize depreciation expenses in the period the assets become available for use, in accordance with Paragraph 71 of IPSAS 17, to reflect accurate balances in the Statement of Financial Position and Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entry to transfer the cost of the brand-new excavator to the Construction and Heavy Equipment account in the amount of P4,300,000.00. We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct a detailed reconciliation of the collections and remittances of loan amortizations under the Multi-purpose Loan and Emergency Loan accounts of the Due to GSIS to determine the root cause of the abnormal/negative balances.  Subsequently, journal entries shall be made to correct the accounts.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office implement stronger internal controls to regularly monitor and reconcile the recorded collections and remittances in the Due to GSIS account.  Coordination with the GSIS should also be established to verify and resolve any discrepancies between the LGU’s records and the GSIS eBCS data promptly.  Further, before processing of refunds to employees, we recommended that the Accounting Office ensure that deductions from their salary were not remitted to the GSIS.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entry to reclassify the balance of funds received for calamity assistance totaling P5,271,000.00 from the Due to LGUs and Due to NGAs accounts to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and record future financial assistance received for disaster related purposes in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. We recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the LDRRM Fund and implement all budgeted projects identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.  Moreover, we recommended that the funds from other LGUs and other sources received in previous years be utilized in accordance with the Memoranda of Agreement.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the amount of P2,493,500.00 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  Moreover, we recommended that the savings from the completed Capital Outlay expenditure items of the LDRRMF be used for new DRRM program/projects/activities of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the concerned LGU officers, henceforth, require the submission of a written authority executed by the intended recipients authorizing other individuals to receive, on their behalf, the amount due, in compliance with Item 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Moreover, we recommended that the Management investigate the claims, which were received by other individuals and the occurrence of inconsistent signatures in order to determine whether the assistance reached the intended recipients of the financial assistance.
Further, we recommended that the Management strengthen its control environment through the creation of LGU operational guidelines, which will guide Accountable Officers and concerned LGU employees in the conduct of distribution of financial assistance and other similar activities and at the same time, will enable effective information dissemination by employing all available platforms to reach all constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management construct a partition for the Municipal Treasury Office to restrict entry to the collecting officer’s area and provide safety deposit boxes/locked drawers to all the Collecting Officers to protect the money and the accountable forms from risks of loss and misuse.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer to orient and require the custodian of the accountable forms to issue the same in strict numerical sequence;
The Barangay Treasurer to remit CTC collections at least once a month to the Municipal Treasury Office.  The custodian of accountable forms must ensure that all collections are remitted and the previously issued CTC booklet is consumed before issuing a new booklet to the Barangay Treasurers.
The collecting officers to prepare the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms at least once a month for submission to the custodian for the preparation of the consolidated RAAF.   Moreover, the custodian shall ensure that in the preparation of monthly CRAAF, all purchased accountable forms are appropriately presented in the report. Finally, the duly approved monthly CRAAF accompanied by the individual RAAFs of the collectors shall be submitted to the Audit Team in the ensuing month.

We recommended that all collecting officers maintain individual cashbook to record their daily collections and remittance to the Municipal Treasurer as prescribed by existing regulations. The use of the cashbook will facilitate monitoring of their respective accountabilities as of a given date.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer timely and completely record all cash advances in the cashbook in accordance with Section 28, Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, to reflect the correct amount of accountability for cash advances and to ensure its proper monitoring and liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks and require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and to furnish the Accounting Office with the original Disbursement Vouchers and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher, following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
Secure Monthly Bank Statements to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements to determine whether checks claimed by the payees were presented for payment, and secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement.
Draw the JEV to restore the cash and liabilities pertaining to the stale checks to present fairly the recorded Cash in Bank and accounts payable accounts.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, undertake the necessary procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2016-005, including the validation of the collectability of dormant receivables and when determined uncollectable, initiate a written request for the authority to write-off the dormant accounts. This will ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations and promote fair presentation of the Other Receivables account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Enjoin the Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 to ensure fair presentation of the Road Networks account in the financial statements;
Require the conduct of inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and the reconciliation of the list of inventory per Local Road Inventory Report and Road Map with those recorded in the Road Networks account. Thereafter, the preparation of Local Road Network Report must be observed yearly; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide adequate disclosure regarding the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements using the format prescribed by the said COA Circular.

The management justified that among the major programs under the disaster preparedness was the improvement of drainage canal. The MPDO explained that unfavorable weather condition resulted to the delay in the implementation of the project.
He further justified that during and after the calamity, several NGA’s and NPO’s extended assistance in the form of cash and relief goods to the constituents of LGU Ballesteros. Such that the Management thought that it is no longer a necessity for them to utilize its 30% quick response fund for relief goods, which resulted to low utilization rate of the fund.
